extraction.one_line_summary: GODEAU David won from the breakaway with a late attack inside final 200m, after SOLAIRE Marc bridged solo from peloton on penultimate climb.
tactical_patterns: Solaire bridged solo from peloton to breakaway on penultimate climb, rare successful tactic revealing exceptional climbing form.
Jumbo-Visma deliberately allowed Vlasov (15th GC, 6:52 down) to attack, showing 6-7 minute gap threshold for GC threats.
Martin attacked GC group with 200m remaining on summit finish, exploiting final kick after rivals marked each other.
Breakaway hesitated until final kilometer despite 16.5km climb, suggesting overconfidence in gap management cost time margin.

rider_signals.SOLAIRE Marc: positive: bridged from peloton solo to join breakaway on category 1 climb, exceptional climbing form mid-Grand Tour
rider_signals.GODEAU David: positive: waited until 125m to attack on cat 1 summit, superior sprint/accelerative power on steep gradients
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: attacked GC group with 200m to go on summit finish, contrarian tactic showing confidence in short power
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Jumbo-Visma allowed him freedom despite being 6:52 down, improved form as race progressed suggests future threat
rider_signals.BENNETT George: negative: 19th on stage despite doing tempo work, poor positioning compared to protected leader Roglic
